3RLAxZniRh.fact: Project discovery unions project folders AND metadata folders. Metadata-only projects (no local project folder) are valid — they represent projects synced to other machines but listable here via metadata. Don't assume every project ID has a local folder.

58cjVvHYjQ.fact: Use Makefile targets for building, testing, and linting — run `make help` to list them. Prefer `make check` over direct go commands.

71h465tvAq.fact: Cross-compile with CGO_ENABLED=0 for pure-Go portability. Known future exception: macOS Finder alias resolution may need cgo — isolate behind a build tag and document.

DMipekjx9c.fact: Test package: use same package to test unexported logic, _test suffix (e.g. package config_test) for black-box API tests. Don't mix both in one file.

IfXmAroTmH.fact: Tests live next to the code — foo_test.go beside foo.go, same directory. No separate test/ tree.

Iuy8mV6wH9.fact: Table-driven tests by default — define cases as []struct{...}, iterate with t.Run(name, ...). One test function per behavior, multiple cases per function.

ZcWGKco0qB.fact: Wrap errors with context using %w — fmt.Errorf("load config: %w", err). Always add context about what was being attempted. Preserve the chain for errors.Is/errors.As.

dq3FcJnQej.fact: Use build-tagged files for platform branching, not runtime.GOOS switches scattered in shared code.

oQIpEweiUe.fact: main.go is only a trampoline — func main() calls cmd.Execute() and nothing else. No logic, no imports beyond cmd.

sximTmkr6a.fact: Version injected via ldflags: go build -ldflags "-X github.com/gorodulin/prj/cmd.version=$(VERSION)". Version from git describe --tags --always --dirty. Never hardcode a version string.

y3WrOlTDSF.fact: Reminder: this codebase distinguishes "project folder" (a folder matching a project ID format inside projects_folder root) from generic folders. If you see "folder" or "directory" in code that specifically deals with project folders, consider whether the naming should reflect that distinction.
